messages.text: What custom tool would you like to build for this space? (e.g., Kanban board, project dashboard, asset tracker)
build a kanban board to track the teams work

**Defining Scope and Simplicity** I'm now zeroing in on defining the scope – it has to be exactly what the user asked for. I'm focusing on radical simplicity for the design, ensuring it's minimal, clean, and intuitive for tracking the team's work. It's crucial that I stick to the strict scope. **Refining Board Specifications** I'm now nailing down the exact specs. The board is definitely a 3-column layout. It will be populated with realistic tasks and assigned to real workspace team members. I will implement their real names and avatar photo URLs using standard HTML `` tags for a clean display. There will be NO bloat or unrequested embellishments. **Prioritizing Functional Code** I'm now completely focused on generating functional code, ditching all placeholders and mock data. It's imperative that I use the synchronizer API for real-time multiplayer functionality, storing state JSON directly. I'm also adhering strictly to the mandate for a single, self-contained `index.html` file, and prioritizing speed and efficiency above all else. **Clarifying the Output Method** I've got it! I will *never* use tools like `write_file` or `bash`. The completed `index.html` code will be delivered directly in a Markdown block in my response from now on. This direct output strategy is a huge time-saver! I can also now start designing the Kanban board with a radically simple and modern design with clean typography and CSS variables. **Implementing Design & Content** Now I'm diving into the specifics of the Kanban board's appearance and task content. I've selected M3 color themes for a modern feel. I'm focusing on populating the board's columns (To Do, In Progress, Done) with realistic tasks pulled from "Project Ollie". I am now assigning tasks to Stephanie Goodson and Chris Carroll, along with their emails and avatar URLs for a functional and accurate display. **Populating More User Data** I'm now integrating more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I've just added Jannica Robertson, Frank Tisellano, Megan Bruce, Ajay Yadav, Belinda Preno, Emily Thomas, and Daniel Giuditta to the team roster. **Adding More Team Members** I'm now integrating even more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I just integrated Zhengrong Ji, Ryan Spohn, Laurence Fahey, Ian Mendiola, and David Wurtz into the team. **Updating Task Statuses** I'm now incorporating tasks related to "Project Ollie" with real statuses and assignments. I've added six new tasks: including Ian Mendiola's work on "Implement Spanner cycle-detection CTE in CreateSubTask" (To Do) and Ryan Spohn's completion of "DDL: Setup FactualFacts & MemoryACLs Spanner tables" (Done). I am also working on setting up multiplayer synchronization. **Adding Drag-and-Drop Capability** I'm now integrating drag-and-drop functionality for enhanced user interaction, aiming for a streamlined experience. I'm focusing on simplicity and ease of use, sticking closely to the three-column layout. I'm choosing HTML5's native drag-and-drop for this reason. I'm focusing on ensuring it is fully self-contained and lightweight, adhering strictly to the system's rules. **Designing Task Input Form** I am now adding an "Add Task" feature, designing a clean input at the top of the "To Do" column. It will include a simple input field and a dropdown to select assignees, displaying their avatar images nicely. I'll stick to the minimalist approach and the original aesthetic of the board. The goal is a beautiful, easy-to-use addition to the Kanban view. I'm focusing on ensuring it's a seamless addition. **Adding More Team Members** I'm now integrating even more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I just integrated Zhengrong Ji, Ryan Spohn, Laurence Fahey, Ian Mendiola, and David Wurtz into the team. **Designing Google-Style Interface** I am now designing the interface and embracing a Google-inspired aesthetic. I'm focusing on a clean, modern look using Tailwind CSS via CDN. I'm implementing the Google Sans font and CSS layout variables to achieve a sleek, lightweight design. The header will include "Project Ollie Task Board" and a dynamic workspace name indicator. **Defining UI Enhancements** I'm now implementing the UI enhancements. I'm focusing on workspace member lists and a live sync indicator, and also incorporating styled columns with headers and task counts. The Kanban cards are taking shape, with outlines, Google Sans text, and assignee sections. I am also implementing action controls and draggable functionality, with special attention to desktop/mobile cross-compatibility. **Constructing Code and Functionality** I'm now fully immersed in constructing the self-contained `index.html` file, complete with embedded styling and scripting. I'm prioritizing simplicity and adherence to the Polaris/M3 system guidelines. I'm focusing on a clean, robust structure with minimal external dependencies, and focusing on a fallback to `localStorage` for sandbox environments, for a fully functional application. I am integrating the required styles for the Google Sans font and colors. I am also adding a new task input form. **Refining Top Bar Implementation** I'm now implementing the top bar design for the Kanban board with a focus on ease of use. I've integrated a task title input, an assignee dropdown, and an "Add Task" button, all styled in accordance with the specifications. The dropdown will be populated dynamically from a refined list of team members. I will be incorporating all features in the `index.html` file, which is well-structured and functional. I am currently working on the javascript code that populates the dropdown. **Adding More Team Members** I'm now integrating even more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I just integrated Daniel Giuditta into the team roster. **Implementing State Handling** I'm now focusing on the real-time interaction features. I'm prioritizing implementing a robust state-handling strategy to prevent disruptions during short-polling updates. I've decided to only redraw elements if the stringified state JSON changes, preventing any interference with the current inputs or focus. I'm exploring deep comparisons to ensure efficient and accurate updates. An elegant, real-time cooperative Kanban board has been designed and implemented in a single self-contained application using Google's **Polaris / Material Design 3 (M3)** specifications and integrated with the Workspace sync service. ### State Update * Thinking about data structure * Building visual component architecture * Writing client synchronization code Here is the complete, self-contained application `index.html`:

summary.text:
**Defining Scope and Simplicity**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the scope – it has to be exactly what the user asked for. I'm focusing on radical simplicity for the design, ensuring it's minimal, clean, and intuitive for tracking the team's work. It's crucial that I stick to the strict scope.

**Refining Board Specifications** I'm now nailing down the exact specs. The board is definitely a 3-column layout. It will be populated with realistic tasks and assigned to real workspace team members. I will implement their real names and avatar photo URLs using standard HTML `` tags for a clean display. There will be NO bloat or unrequested embellishments.

**Prioritizing Functional Code**

I'm now completely focused on generating functional code, ditching all placeholders and mock data. It's imperative that I use the synchronizer API for real-time multiplayer functionality, storing state JSON directly. I'm also adhering strictly to the mandate for a single, self-contained `index.html` file, and prioritizing speed and efficiency above all else.

**Clarifying the Output Method**

I've got it! I will *never* use tools like `write_file` or `bash`. The completed `index.html` code will be delivered directly in a Markdown block in my response from now on. This direct output strategy is a huge time-saver! I can also now start designing the Kanban board with a radically simple and modern design with clean typography and CSS variables.

**Implementing Design & Content**

Now I'm diving into the specifics of the Kanban board's appearance and task content. I've selected M3 color themes for a modern feel. I'm focusing on populating the board's columns (To Do, In Progress, Done) with realistic tasks pulled from "Project Ollie". I am now assigning tasks to Stephanie Goodson and Chris Carroll, along with their emails and avatar URLs for a functional and accurate display.

**Populating More User Data**

I'm now integrating more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I've just added Jannica Robertson, Frank Tisellano, Megan Bruce, Ajay Yadav, Belinda Preno, Emily Thomas, and Daniel Giuditta to the team roster.

**Adding More Team Members**

I'm now integrating even more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I just integrated Zhengrong Ji, Ryan Spohn, Laurence Fahey, Ian Mendiola, and David Wurtz into the team.

**Updating Task Statuses**

I'm now incorporating tasks related to "Project Ollie" with real statuses and assignments. I've added six new tasks: including Ian Mendiola's work on "Implement Spanner cycle-detection CTE in CreateSubTask" (To Do) and Ryan Spohn's completion of "DDL: Setup FactualFacts & MemoryACLs Spanner tables" (Done). I am also working on setting up multiplayer synchronization.

**Adding Drag-and-Drop Capability**

I'm now integrating drag-and-drop functionality for enhanced user interaction, aiming for a streamlined experience. I'm focusing on simplicity and ease of use, sticking closely to the three-column layout. I'm choosing HTML5's native drag-and-drop for this reason. I'm focusing on ensuring it is fully self-contained and lightweight, adhering strictly to the system's rules.

**Designing Task Input Form**

I am now adding an "Add Task" feature, designing a clean input at the top of the "To Do" column. It will include a simple input field and a dropdown to select assignees, displaying their avatar images nicely. I'll stick to the minimalist approach and the original aesthetic of the board. The goal is a beautiful, easy-to-use addition to the Kanban view. I'm focusing on ensuring it's a seamless addition.

**Adding More Team Members**

I'm now integrating even more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I just integrated Zhengrong Ji, Ryan Spohn, Laurence Fahey, Ian Mendiola, and David Wurtz into the team.

**Designing Google-Style Interface**

I am now designing the interface and embracing a Google-inspired aesthetic. I'm focusing on a clean, modern look using Tailwind CSS via CDN. I'm implementing the Google Sans font and CSS layout variables to achieve a sleek, lightweight design. The header will include "Project Ollie Task Board" and a dynamic workspace name indicator.

**Defining UI Enhancements**

I'm now implementing the UI enhancements. I'm focusing on workspace member lists and a live sync indicator, and also incorporating styled columns with headers and task counts. The Kanban cards are taking shape, with outlines, Google Sans text, and assignee sections. I am also implementing action controls and draggable functionality, with special attention to desktop/mobile cross-compatibility.

**Constructing Code and Functionality**

I'm now fully immersed in constructing the self-contained `index.html` file, complete with embedded styling and scripting. I'm prioritizing simplicity and adherence to the Polaris/M3 system guidelines. I'm focusing on a clean, robust structure with minimal external dependencies, and focusing on a fallback to `localStorage` for sandbox environments, for a fully functional application. I am integrating the required styles for the Google Sans font and colors. I am also adding a new task input form.

**Refining Top Bar Implementation**

I'm now implementing the top bar design for the Kanban board with a focus on ease of use. I've integrated a task title input, an assignee dropdown, and an "Add Task" button, all styled in accordance with the specifications. The dropdown will be populated dynamically from a refined list of team members. I will be incorporating all features in the `index.html` file, which is well-structured and functional. I am currently working on the javascript code that populates the dropdown.

**Adding More Team Members**

I'm now integrating even more user data, specifically names, email addresses, and avatar URLs for additional team members. I just integrated Daniel Giuditta into the team roster.

**Implementing State Handling**

I'm now focusing on the real-time interaction features. I'm prioritizing implementing a robust state-handling strategy to prevent disruptions during short-polling updates. I've decided to only redraw elements if the stringified state JSON changes, preventing any interference with the current inputs or focus. I'm exploring deep comparisons to ensure efficient and accurate updates.
